title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: SHMI Organic Inputs sub‑index quantifies frequency biological inputs—organic amendments animal presence—across rotation. rotation year assigned weighted organic‑input presence based user‑specified weights amendments animals. final score scaled 0–100, higher values indicate frequent biological inputs. sub‑index captures management practices add organic matter, stimulate microbial activity, contribute soil biological functioning.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: management unit ii, sequence rotation years constructed: year∈{rot_start_yri,…,rot_end_yri} \text{year} \\{\text{rot_start_yr}_i, \ldots, \text{rot_end_yr}_i\} defines evaluation window organic inputs.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: Organic amendments identified amendment table: rows SA_cat="Organic"\text{SA_cat} = \text{"Organic"} contribute. Amendment years extracted : year=year(SA_date) \text{year} = \text{year}(\text{SA_date}) amendment year marked: amend_presenti,y=1 \text{amend_present}_{,y} = 1 years receive 0.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: Animal presence identified animal table: year=year(AD_start_date) \text{year} = \text{year}(\text{AD_start_date}) year animal presence marked: ani_presenti,y=1 \text{ani_present}_{,y} = 1 years receive 0.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: rotation year: weighted_inputi,y=wamend⋅amend_presenti,y+wanimal⋅ani_presenti,y \text{weighted\_input}_{,y} = w_{\text{amend}} \cdot \text{amend_present}_{,y} + w_{\text{animal}} \cdot \text{ani_present}_{,y} Default weights: wamend=1w_{\text{amend}} = 1 wanimal=1w_{\text{animal}} = 1 Weights allow tuning based local priorities empirical optimization.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: Let YiY_i number rotation years management unit ii. Total weighted inputs across rotation: total_weightedi=∑yweighted_inputi,y \text{total_weighted}_i = \sum_{y} \text{weighted_input}_{,y} Annualized frequency: events_per_yeari=total_weightediYi \text{events_per_year}_i = \frac{\text{total_weighted}_i}{Y_i} Yi=0Y_i = 0, value defaults 0.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: management units zero events: OrgInputsi=0 \text{OrgInputs}_i = 0 Otherwise, values rescaled [0,100][0,100] using min–max transformation: OrgInputsi=100×events_per_yeari−min(events_per_year)max(events_per_year)−min(events_per_year) \text{OrgInputs}_i = 100 \times \frac{\text{events_per_year}_i - \min(\text{events_per_year})} {\max(\text{events_per_year}) - \min(\text{events_per_year})} ensures comparability across systems different event frequencies.

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index
text: 0 → organic amendments animal presence across rotation 100 → Highest observed frequency biological inputs Weights allow emphasizing amendments, animals, Captures management intensity related organic matter biological activity

title: SHMI Organic Inputs Sub‑index

title: SHMI Background
text: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) created fill gap two worlds: management‑based soil health frameworks, emphasize decisions practices measurement‑based soil health frameworks, emphasize biological chemical indicators SHMI intentionally latent variable model, covariance‑driven index, outcome‑optimized score. Instead, designed, portable, theory‑aligned index encodes management principles directly structure. SHMI’s purpose provide: transparent, reproducible representation management intensity stable index change every new dataset tool can validated soil health outcomes without derived separation design validation central SHMI’s philosophy.

title: SHMI Background
text: SHMI built four sub‑indices, representing major dimension soil health management: Organic Inputs Cover Diversity Inverse Disturbance represent widely recognized levers improving soil function: organic matter additions continuous living cover plant diversity reduced disturbance pillars align NRCS guidance, soil health literature, regenerative agriculture frameworks.

title: SHMI Background
text: SHMI includes tunable parameters allow researchers explore alternative conceptual designs without altering underlying philosophy: weights sub‑index seasonal cover weights diversity metric choice (Hill number) maximum allowable disturbance (stir cap) parameters define design space, data‑driven optimization problem.

what: Bayesian Optimization as a Design Tool
title: SHMI Background
text: Bayesian optimization can used explore design space, SHMI “fit” outcomes. Optimization helps identify conceptually coherent parameter sets align soil health theory.

title: SHMI Background
text: Interpretation: Grey boxes represent tunable settings Green boxes represent sub‑indices diagram illustrates tunable parameters influence sub‑indices sub‑indices combine SHMI.

title: SHMI Background
text: SHMI explicitly avoids: latent variable modeling PCA, factor analysis, covariance‑based weighting machine‑learned outcome‑optimized indices site‑specific calibration black‑box transformations SHMI portable, theory‑driven index, statistical artifact.

title: SHMI Background
text: SHMI proxy soil health. predictor soil health outcomes, grounded management theory. distinction matters: SHMI designed management principles Soil health measured biological, chemical, physical indicators Validation tests whether design aligns observed outcomes preserves conceptual clarity prevents circular reasoning.

title: SHMI Background
text: Validation performed SHMI constructed, using independent soil health datasets. Validation asks: SHMI correlate soil health indicators? predict biological functional outcomes? behave consistently across regions systems? SHMI tuned outcomes, validation genuine test theory.

title: SHMI Background
text: SHMI : designed index grounded management theory built four sub‑indices parameterized tunable conceptual settings validated independent soil health data conceptual design ensures SHMI remains transparent, portable, scientifically defensible. full SHMI workflow, see SHMI Overview article.

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: SHMI Cover sub‑index quantifies proportion days within crop rotation living plant cover present, explicit weighting four meteorological seasons. indicator reflects ecological principle plant cover contributes differently soil health depending time year (e.g., winter cover disproportionately valuable erosion control). Cover computed management unit (MGT_combo) using daily crop‑presence data rotation start/end dates. final score ranges 0 100.

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: day d rotation window management unit , input data include: crop_presenti,d∈{0,1}\text{crop_present}_{,d} \\{0,1\} CD_namei,d(used force fallow → 0)\text{CD_name}_{,d} \; \text{(used force fallow → 0)} datei,d\text{date}_{,d} MGT_comboi\text{MGT_combo}_{} multiple crops appear day crop_presenti,d={1if crop present0otherwise \text{crop\_present}_{,d} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{crop present} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} Days CD_name="fallow"\text{CD_name} = \text{"fallow"} always set 0.

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: day assigned season based calendar month: season(d)={winterm∈{12,1,2}springm∈{3,4,5}summerm∈{6,7,8}fallm∈{9,10,11} \text{season}(d) = \begin{cases} \text{winter} & m \\{12,1,2\} \\ \text{spring} & m \\{3,4,5\} \\ \text{summer} & m \\{6,7,8\} \\ \text{fall}   & m \\{9,10,11\} \end{cases}

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: management unit ii season ss: plant_daysi,s=∑d∈scrop_presenti,d \text{plant_days}_{,s} = \sum_{d \s} \text{crop_present}_{,d} days_possiblei,s=#{d∈s} \text{days_possible}_{,s} = \#\{ d \s \} yields seasonal totals : plant_daysi,winter\text{plant_days}_{,\text{winter}} days_possiblei,winter\text{days_possible}_{,\text{winter}}

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: Seasonal cover proportions bounded [0,1][0,1]: propi,s={plant_daysi,sdays_possiblei,sif days_possiblei,s>00otherwise \text{prop}_{,s} = \begin{cases} \dfrac{\text{plant_days}_{,s}}{\text{days_possible}_{,s}} & \text{} \text{days_possible}_{,s} > 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} ensures rotations lacking days season (e.g., short rotations) produce NaN values.

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: User‑supplied seasonal weights: wwinterw_{\text{winter}} wspringw_{\text{spring}} wsummerw_{\text{summer}} wfallw_{\text{fall}} normalized sum 1: ws*=wswwinter+wspring+wsummer+wfall w_s^{*} = \frac{w_s}{\,w_{\text{winter}} + w_{\text{spring}} + w_{\text{summer}} + w_{\text{fall}}\,} guarantees interpretability prevents scaling artifacts.

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: SHMI Cover sub‑index management unit ii : Coveri=100×(wwinter*propi,winter+wspring*propi,spring+wsummer*propi,summer+wfall*propi,fall) \text{Cover}_i = 100 \times \left( w_{\text{winter}}^{*}\,\text{prop}_{,\text{winter}} + w_{\text{spring}}^{*}\,\text{prop}_{,\text{spring}} + w_{\text{summer}}^{*}\,\text{prop}_{,\text{summer}} + w_{\text{fall}}^{*}\,\text{prop}_{,\text{fall}} \right) construction: 0≤Coveri≤100 0 \le \text{Cover}_i \le 100

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: 0 → plant cover season 100 → Continuous cover seasons, weighted seasonal importance Seasonal weights allow tuning local soil‑health priorities (e.g., winter erosion control, summer evapotranspiration buffering)

title: SHMI Cover Sub‑index
text: data frame one row per management unit:

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: SHMI Disturbance sub‑index quantifies mechanical soil disturbance using mechanistic soil‑mixing model based mixing efficiency tillage depth. Disturbance computed annual scale averaged across rotation. final score expressed inverse disturbance, scaled 0–100, higher values indicate less mechanical disturbance. formulation captures intensity cumulative penetration tillage operations, reflecting repeated passes reduce effective depth subsequent disturbance.

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: Daily disturbance data management unit ii include: SD_mixeffi,d\text{SD_mixeff}_{,d} — mixing efficiency (0–1) SD_depth_cmi,d\text{SD_depth_cm}_{,d} — tillage depth cm (capped 30) datei,d\text{date}_{,d} — calendar date MGT_comboi\text{MGT_combo}_i Rotation bounds provide: rot_starti\text{rot_start}_i rot_endi\text{rot_end}_i Depth normalized : SD_depth_cm=min(SD_depth_cm,30) \text{SD_depth_cm} = \min(\text{SD_depth_cm}, 30)

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: Within year, disturbance events ordered : Management unit Year Depth Mixing efficiency ensures deeper, aggressive operations applied first cumulative‑mixing model.

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: disturbance event ii:

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: MEi=SD_mixeffi×SD_depth_cmi ME_i = \text{SD_mixeff}_i \times \text{SD_depth_cm}_i

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: cumMEi=∑j<iMEj \text{cumME}_i = \sum_{j < } ME_j

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: effective penetration disturbance event : Tt,=SD_mixeffi×max(0,SD_depth_cmi−cumMEi) T_{t,} = \text{SD_mixeff}_i \times \max\left(0,\; \text{SD_depth_cm}_i - \text{cumME}_i \right) Normalized 30‑cm reference depth: Tt,inorm=Tt,i30 T_{t,}^{\text{norm}} = \frac{T_{t,}}{30}

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: Annual disturbance sum normalized penetrations: Ttannual=∑iTt,inorm T_t^{\text{annual}} = \sum_i T_{t,}^{\text{norm}}

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: Annual inverse disturbance : Ttinv=1−Ttannual T_t^{\text{inv}} = 1 - T_t^{\text{annual}} Values 0 1 expected capped model.

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: Annual inverse disturbance averaged across rotation years: InvDisti=100×mean(Tt,yinv) \text{InvDist}_i = 100 \times \text{mean}\left(T_{t,y}^{\text{inv}}\right) management unit disturbance events, : InvDisti=100 \text{InvDist}_i = 100

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: 100 → mechanical disturbance 0 → Maximum possible disturbance (deep, repeated, high‑efficiency tillage) metric captures depth cumulative mixing, penalizing repeated passes 30‑cm cap ensures comparability across systems

title: SHMI Disturbance Sub‑index
text: data frame one row per management unit:

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: SHMI Diversity sub‑index quantifies diversity crops grown across entire rotation, accounting species identity time field. Diversity computed using entropy‑based Hill numbers, generalize richness, Shannon entropy, Simpson entropy unified framework. algorithm expands crop mixtures, sums plant‑days across years rotation, computes species proportions, applies selected Hill‑number order (default: Simpson entropy, q=2q = 2). final score scaled 0–100 using user‑specified theoretical maximum (default: max_div = 8).

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: Daily crop presence summarized management unit ii, crop sequence number, crop name, year: daysi,j,y=∑dcrop_presenti,j,y,d \text{days}_{,j,y} = \sum_{d} \text{crop_present}_{,j,y,d} : ii = management unit jj = crop mixture yy = year dd = day Records missing crop sequence numbers excluded.

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: Crop mixtures expanded individual species: Mixtures like "+ B" become species B Placeholder mixtures like "3-species" becomespecies1,species2,species3\text{species}_1, \text{species}_2, \text{species}_3 expansion, species inherits plant‑days mixture. Fallow treated zero diversity: days=0if species = "fallow" \text{days} = 0 \quad \text{species = "fallow"}

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: Plant‑days summed across years rotation: daysi,s=∑ydaysi,s,y \text{days}_{,s} = \sum_{y} \text{days}_{,s,y} Species proportions computed: pi,s=daysi,s∑s′daysi,s′ p_{,s} = \frac{\text{days}_{,s}}{\sum_{s'} \text{days}_{,s'}}

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: diversity metric DD depends Hill‑number order qq:

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: D=∑(ps>0) D = \sum (p_s > 0)

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: D=−∑pslogps D = -\sum p_s \log p_s

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: D=−log(∑ps2) D = -\log \left( \sum p_s^2 \right) result capped theoretical maximum: D←min(D,max_div) D \leftarrow \min(D, \text{max_div})

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: Richness scaled linearly: Diversityraw=Dmax_div \text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}} = \frac{D}{\text{max_div}} Entropy‑based metrics scaled log maximum: Diversityraw=Dlog(max_div) \text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}} = \frac{D}{\log(\text{max_div})} Final score: Diversity=100×min(Diversityraw,1) \text{Diversity} = 100 \times \min(\text{Diversity}_{\text{raw}}, 1)

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: 0 → Monoculture rotation (fallow‑) 100 → Maximum theoretical diversity (e.g., 8 equally represented species) Entropy‑based metrics reward richness evenness Mixture crops contribute proportionally diversity

title: SHMI Diversity Sub‑index
text: data frame one row per management unit:

title: SHMI Overview
text: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) provides quantitative, rotation‑scale assessment soil‑health management practices. integrates four biologically grounded sub‑indices: Cover — seasonal plant presence Diversity — rotation‑scale crop diversity (Hill numbers) Inverse Disturbance — mechanistic mixing‑efficiency × depth metric Organic Inputs — organic amendments animal presence vignette walks standard SHMI workflow using official Excel template.

title: SHMI Overview
text: SHMI begins standardized Excel workbook containing following sheets: Mgt_Unit Crop_Diversity Soil_Disturbance Amendment_Diversity Animal_Diversity Download blank template: : Open SHMI_template.xlsx Excel Enter management data sheet Save completed file (e.g., "my_shmi_inputs.xlsx") Prepare inputs: prepare_shmi_inputs(): validates required sheets columns harmonizes crop windows constructs rotation bounds builds daily grids computes daily disturbance assembles amendment animal events returned object named list containing: rot_bounds crop_harmonized daily daily_dist amend animal metadata feed directly build_shmi().

title: SHMI Overview
text: inputs prepared: output includes: Cover Diversity InvDist Animals SHMI (weighted composite) Plus metadata: settings_used expert_mode timestamp shmi_version

title: SHMI Overview
text: default, SHMI uses official national settings (locked mode). explore scenarios conduct research, may override settings: expert_mode = TRUE, SHMI values comparable national scale.
code: custom <- list(   # cover   w_winter = 0.25,   w_spring = 0.25,   w_summer = 0.25,   w_fall   = 0.25,    # diversity   hill    = 2,   max_div = 8,    # SHMI pillar weights   w_cover = 0.25,   w_div   = 0.25,   w_dist  = 0.25,   w_ani   = 0.25 )  shmi_exp <- build_shmi(inputs, settings = custom, expert_mode = TRUE)

title: SHMI Overview
text: may compute sub‑indices individually:

title: SHMI Overview
text: final SHMI score reflects: year‑round cover crop diversity reduced soil disturbance organic inputs Higher SHMI values indicate management systems supportive soil health.

title: SHMI Overview
text: SHMI package provides fast, reproducible, biologically grounded framework quantifying soil‑health management across diverse systems. details: ?prepare_shmi_inputs ?build_shmi ?compute_cover ?compute_diversity ?compute_disturbance ?compute_orginput

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) integrates four management indices—Cover, Diversity, Inverse Disturbance, Animals—single 0–100 score. pillar computed harmonized rotation‑scale inputs combined using official national SHMI weights (locked mode) user‑specified weights (expert mode). SHMI designed : Mechanistic (built daily annual process‑based inputs) Rotation‑scale (single‑year snapshots) Comparable across systems (locked mode) Flexible research (expert mode)

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: SHMI can computed two modes:

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: Uses official national SHMI settings: Seasonal cover weights Hill number max diversity Amendment/animal weights Sub-index weights User‑supplied settings ignored.

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: User settings override defaults. Resulting SHMI values comparable national scale.

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: computation, input list prepare_shmi_inputs() validated: Required tables present Required columns present Valid date formats duplicated daily rows missing MGT_combo Harmonized crop windows consistent validation fails, SHMI computation stops explicit error messages.

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: pillar computed using dedicated sub‑index function: CoverCoveri=compute_cover(...)\text{Cover}_i = \text{compute_cover}(...) DiversityDiversityi=compute_diversity(...)\text{Diversity}_i = \text{compute_diversity}(...) Inverse DisturbanceInvDisti=compute_disturbance(...)\text{InvDist}_i = \text{compute_disturbance}(...) Organic Inputs (Animals + Amendments)Animalsi=compute_orginput(...)\text{Animals}_i = \text{compute_orginput}(...) sub‑index already scaled 0–100.

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: Let four sub‑indices management unit ii : CiC_i = Cover DiD_i = Diversity IiI_i = Inverse Disturbance AiA_i = Animals Let pillar weights : wcoverw_{\text{cover}} wdivw_{\text{div}} wdistw_{\text{dist}} waniw_{\text{ani}} Weights normalized: ws*=wswcover+wdiv+wdist+wani w_s^{*} = \frac{w_s}{w_{\text{cover}} + w_{\text{div}} + w_{\text{dist}} + w_{\text{ani}}} final SHMI score : SHMIi=wcover*Ci+wdiv*Di+wdist*Ii+wani*Ai \text{SHMI}_i = w_{\text{cover}}^{*} C_i + w_{\text{div}}^{*} D_i + w_{\text{dist}}^{*} I_i + w_{\text{ani}}^{*} A_i construction: 0≤SHMIi≤100 0 \le \text{SHMI}_i \le 100

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: final output tidy data frame: Additional metadata returned: settings_used — actual settings applied expert_mode — TRUE/FALSE shmi_version — version string timestamp — computation time

title: Soil Health Management Index (SHMI)
text: SHMI = 0 → cover, diversity, high disturbance, organic inputs SHMI = 100 → Continuous cover, high diversity, disturbance, frequent organic inputs Pillar weights reflect national priorities locked mode Expert mode enables research flexibility

title: SHMI
text: SHMI converts multi‑year crop, disturbance, amendment records standardized soil‑health management scores. SHMI R package provides complete, reproducible workflow computing Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) standardized Excel workbooks R‑native example data. SHMI integrates four management sub‑indices: Cover — seasonal plant presence Diversity — rotation‑scale crop diversity (Hill numbers) Inverse Disturbance — mechanistic mixing‑efficiency × depth metric Organic Inputs — organic amendments animal presence package includes: robust Excel ingestion validation biologically realistic crop‑window harmonization fast vectorized daily‑grid construction mechanistic disturbance modeling rotation‑scale aggregation official national SHMI settings (locked mode) expert‑mode overrides research scenario analysis

title: SHMI
text: Agricultural management multidimensional, many soil health indicators respond long‑term patterns, single‑year practices. SHMI provides: rotation‑scale measure management intensity standardized, reproducible workflow diverse datasets transparent scoring system grounded ecological function consistent template data entry QA/QC Tools help researchers practitioners compare systems, evaluate interventions, link management soil outcomes

title: SHMI
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: SHMI
text: (Excel required) built‑shmi_example dataset already processed prepare_shmi_inputs(), can passed directly build_shmi(). ideal teaching, demos, unit tests.
code: library(SHMI)  # Load the built-in example dataset data(shmi_example)  # Compute SHMI result <- build_shmi(shmi_example)  result$indicator_df

what: 2. Download the fully populated example Excel file
title: SHMI
text: file contains realistic management data ready use immediately.
code: library(SHMI)  # Save example Excel file to working directory download_shmi_example("SHMI_example.xlsx")  # Prepare inputs inputs <- prepare_shmi_inputs("SHMI_example.xlsx")  # Compute SHMI result <- build_shmi(inputs)  result$indicator_df

title: SHMI
text: (standard workflow analyzing management data) Important: template intentionally blank. contains required sheets column structure, management data. must fill crop, disturbance, amendment, animal records running prepare_shmi_inputs(). prepare_shmi_inputs() performs full validation return clear, actionable error messages : required sheets columns missing dates invalid MGT_combo identifiers inconsistent crop windows malformed disturbance amendment entries incomplete workflow one users follow computing SHMI fields research datasets
code: library(SHMI)  # Download a blank SHMI template to a known location path <- "myDir/SHMI_template.xlsx" download_shmi_template(path = path)  # (1) Open "myDir/SHMI_template.xlsx" in Excel # (2) Enter your management data into each sheet # (3) Save the completed file as "myDir/my_shmi_inputs.xlsx"  # Prepare inputs user_file <- "myDir/my_shmi_inputs.xlsx" inputs <- prepare_shmi_inputs(user_file)  # Compute SHMI result <- build_shmi(inputs)  result$indicator_df

what: Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
title: Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
text: Computes Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) management unit (`MGT_combo`) using harmonized inputs produced prepare_shmi_inputs(). SHMI weighted composite four sub-indices: cover, diversity, inverse disturbance, organic inputs (amendments + animals). default, function uses official national SHMI settings (locked mode). expert mode, users may override settings, resulting scores longer comparable national SHMI scale.

title: Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
text: shmi_inputs list returned prepare_shmi_inputs(), containing minimum: rot_bounds — rotation start/end dates crop_harmonized — harmonized crop windows daily — daily crop presence table daily_dist — daily disturbance table amend — amendment events animal — animal events settings Optional named list SHMI settings (seasonal cover weights, Hill number, max diversity, pillar weights, etc.). Ignored unless expert_mode = TRUE. Missing elements filled official defaults. expert_mode Logical; FALSE (default), SHMI computed using official national settings custom settings ignored. TRUE, custom settings allowed resulting SHMI values comparable national SHMI scale.

title: Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
text: list : indicator_df — data frame columns:       MGT_combo, SHMI, Cover, Diversity,       InvDist, Animals settings_used — settings actually applied expert_mode — logical flag shmi_version — version string reproducibility timestamp — computation time

title: Build SHMI Scores from Prepared Inputs — build_shmi
text: SHMI computation proceeds five stages: Settings:     locked mode, official national SHMI settings always used.     expert mode, user-supplied settings override defaults. Input validation:     Ensures required elements prepare_shmi_inputs()     present. Pillar computation: Cover — via compute_cover() Diversity — via compute_diversity() Inverse disturbance — via compute_disturbance() Organic inputs — via compute_orginput() Weighted combination:     Sub-indices normalized weights sum 1, combined     single SHMI score:     $$       SHMI = w_{cover} \cdot Cover +              w_{div}   \cdot Diversity +              w_{dist}  \cdot InvDist +              w_{ani}   \cdot Animals     $$ Output assembly:     Returns tidy data frame SHMI scores along metadata describing     settings used computation timestamp. Additionally, computing SHMI sub-indices, function automatically validates internal SHMI data list using validate_shmi_input(). validator checks structural completeness (e.g., required tables, required columns, valid date types, duplicated daily rows, missing MGT_combo values) ensures harmonized data produced prepare_shmi_inputs() consistent ready SHMI computation. validation fails, execution stops immediately explicit error messages. Users must correct input data Excel file re-running build_shmi().

what: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
title: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
text: Calculates SHMI cover indicator management unit (`MGT_combo`) using daily crop presence data rotation bounds. Cover computed weighted average seasonal plant‑days, season (winter, spring, summer, fall) contributes user‑specified weight. Seasonal plant‑days normalized expected number days per season (one‑quarter rotation length), final cover score scaled 0–100.

title: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
code: compute_cover(   daily,   rot_bounds,   w_winter = 0.13,   w_spring = 0.129,   w_summer = 0.513,   w_fall = 0.227 )

title: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
text: daily daily data frame produced prepare_shmi_inputs(), containing least: MGT_combo — management unit identifier date — calendar date crop_present — 1 crop present, 0 otherwise CD_name — crop name (used treat "fallow" 0 cover) rot_bounds data frame rotation start end dates MGT_combo, containing: MGT_combo rot_start rot_end w_winter Numeric weight winter cover (default 0.130). w_spring Numeric weight spring cover (default 0.129). w_summer Numeric weight summer cover (default 0.513). w_fall Numeric weight fall cover (default 0.227).

title: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
text: data frame one row per MGT_combo single column: Cover — SHMI cover score (0–100)

title: Compute the SHMI Cover Sub-index (Season‑Weighted Plant Presence) — compute_cover
text: algorithm proceeds four steps: Assign daily record season based calendar month. Sum plant‑days within season MGT_combo. Normalize seasonal totals one‑quarter rotation length. Apply seasonal weights scale final cover score 0–100. Days CD_name == "fallow" treated zero cover.

what: Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
title: Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
text: Calculates SHMI disturbance sub-index management unit (`MGT_combo`) using mechanistic soil-mixing model based mixing efficiency tillage depth. Disturbance computed *annual* scale averaged across rotation. final inverse-disturbance score scaled 0–100.

title: Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
text: daily_dist daily disturbance table produced prepare_shmi_inputs(), containing: MGT_combo — management unit identifier date — calendar date SD_mixeff — mixing efficiency (0–1) SD_depth_cm — tillage depth cm (already capped 30) rot_bounds data frame rotation bounds management unit, containing: MGT_combo rot_start rot_end

title: Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
text: data frame : MGT_combo InvDist — inverse disturbance score (0–100)

title: Compute Mechanistic Inverse Disturbance (Mixing-Efficiency × Depth Metric) — compute_disturbance
text: Disturbance computed using mechanistic soil-mixing model: Depth normalization:     Tillage depth converted cm capped 30 cm. Annual ordering:     Within year, disturbance events ordered depth mixing     efficiency. Cumulative mechanical energy:     $$ ME_i = SD\_mixeff_i \times SD\_depth\_cm_i $$     $$ cumME_i = \sum_{j < } ME_j $$ Profile penetration (T_t):     $$ T_{t,} = SD\_mixeff_i \times \max(0, SD\_depth\_cm_i - cumME_i) $$     $$ T_{t,}^{norm} = T_{t,} / 30 $$ Annual disturbance:     $$ T_t^{annual} = \sum_i T_{t,}^{norm} $$ Inverse disturbance:     $$ T_t^{inv} = 1 - T_t^{annual} $$ Rotation average:     Annual inverse disturbance averaged across rotation years     scaled 0–100. management unit disturbance events, inverse disturbance defined 100 (disturbance).

what: Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
title: Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
text: Calculates SHMI diversity sub-index management unit (`MGT_combo`) using daily crop presence data harmonized crop windows. Diversity computed *rotation scale* expanding species mixtures, summing plant-days across years, applying Hill-number diversity metrics entropy form (Shannon Simpson). final diversity score scaled 0–100 using user-specified theoretical maximum.

title: Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
text: crop_harmonized data frame produced prepare_shmi_inputs(), containing one row per crop event harmonized start/end dates mixture names. daily daily data frame prepare_shmi_inputs(), containing: MGT_combo — management unit identifier date — calendar date CD_seq_num — crop sequence number CD_name — crop mixture name crop_present — 1 crop present, 0 otherwise hill Hill-number order. Supported values: 0: species richness 1: Shannon entropy 2: Simpson entropy (entropy form) Default 2. max_div theoretical maximum diversity used scaling final index 0–100. richness (hill = 0), maximum number species. entropy-based metrics, scaling uses log(max_div).

title: Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
text: data frame : MGT_combo Diversity — rotation-scale diversity score (0–100)

title: Compute Rotation-Scale Crop Diversity (Entropy-Based Hill Numbers) — compute_diversity
text: algorithm proceeds four stages: Daily plant-days: Sum daily crop presence     MGT_combo × CD_seq_num × CD_name × year. Mixture expansion:     Mixtures "+ B" split individual species.     Placeholder mixtures like "3-species" expanded     species_1, species_2, species_3. Rotation-scale plant-days:     Plant-days summed across years rotation species. Entropy-based diversity:     Species proportions \(p_i\) computed diversity calculated : Richness: \(D = \sum (p_i > 0)\) Shannon entropy: \(D = -\sum p_i \log p_i\) Simpson entropy: \(D = -\log \sum p_i^2\) result capped max_div scaled 0–100.

what: Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
title: Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
text: Calculates SHMI organic-inputs indicator management unit (`MGT_combo`) combining amendment events animal events across rotation. year rotation assigned weighted organic-input presence based user-specified weights amendments animals. final score scaled 0–100.

title: Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
text: rot_bounds data frame prepare_shmi_inputs() containing rotation-year bounds management unit, columns: MGT_combo rot_start_yr rot_end_yr amend data frame amendment events (Amendment_Diversity sheet), containing: MGT_combo SA_date — amendment date SA_cat — amendment category (e.g., "Organic") rows SA_cat == "Organic" contribute index. animal data frame animal events (Animal_Diversity sheet), containing: MGT_combo AD_start_date — start animal presence w_amend Numeric weight applied amendment events (default 1). w_animal Numeric weight applied animal events (default 1).

title: Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
text: data frame : MGT_combo events_per_year — weighted organic-input frequency Animals — final SHMI organic-input score (0–100)

title: Compute the Organic Inputs Sub-index (Amendments + Animals) — compute_orginput
text: algorithm proceeds six steps: Rotation-year grid:     MGT_combo, construct sequence years     rot_start_yr rot_end_yr. Amendment events:     Identify years organic amendments mark     amend_present = 1. Animal events:     Identify years animal presence mark     ani_present = 1. Weighted presence:     year rotation:     $$ \text{weighted\_input} = w_{\text{amend}} \cdot \text{amend\_present}            + w_{\text{animal}} \cdot \text{ani\_present} $$ Rotation-average:     Weighted inputs summed across rotation divided number     rotation years. Scaling:     rotation-average rescaled 0–100 using     scales::rescale().

title: Download the example SHMI Excel file — download_shmi_example
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Save example file to Desktop download_shmi_example("~/Desktop")  # Run the full SHMI workflow example_file <- file.path("~/Desktop", "SHMI_example_1.xlsx") inputs <- prepare_shmi_inputs(example_file) result <- build_shmi(inputs) head(result$indicator_df) } # }

what: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
text: Reads, validates, harmonizes, expands input sheets required compute Soil Health Management Index (SHMI). function official entry point SHMI data preparation produces standardized list objects used directly build_shmi().

title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
code: prepare_shmi_inputs(   path,   exclude = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   start_date_override = NULL,   end_date_override = NULL )

title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
text: path Path SHMI Excel workbook. Must contain standard SHMI sheets: Mgt_Unit, Crop_Diversity, Soil_Disturbance, Amendment_Diversity, Animal_Diversity. Sheets may empty; empty sheets safely ignored. exclude Optional character vector MGT_combo identifiers exclude processing. Default NULL. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints progress messages describing sheet ingestion, validation steps, grid construction. start_date_override Optional Date date‑coercible value. supplied, crop, disturbance, amendment, animal events occurring date removed, rotation bounds clipped accordingly. end_date_override Optional Date date‑coercible value. supplied, events occurring date removed, rotation bounds clipped accordingly.

title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
text: named list containing: rot_bounds Rotation start/end dates MGT_combo. crop_harmonized One row per crop event harmonized         start/end dates. daily Daily crop‑presence grid (one row per day). daily_dist Daily disturbance table mixing efficiency         depth (cm). mgt Management‑unit metadata. crop Validated crop event table. dist Disturbance event table. amend Amendment event table. animal Animal event table.

title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
text: function performs: robust Excel ingestion sheet‑level validation management‑unit filtering crop‑level biological validation (chronology, annual/perennial rules) harmonization crop windows across mixtures construction rotation bounds daily grid expansion (fast vectorized implementation) mechanistic daily disturbance processing (mixing efficiency × depth) assembly amendment animal event tables function enforces biologically realistic crop windows, including: annual crops must terminate within year perennials may span years must follow valid chronology mixtures collapsed event‑level windows Rotation bounds computed available event types (crop, disturbance, amendment, animal). Empty sheets contribute bounds. Daily grids generated using fully vectorized expansion, ensuring extremely fast performance even large datasets. Additionally, function automatically performs front‑end validation Excel input file using validate_excel_input(). validator checks required sheets, required columns, valid date formats, consistent MGT_combo values, malformed entries ingestion harmonization occurs. validation fails, execution stops immediately clear, actionable error messages. Users must correct Excel file re‑running prepare_shmi_inputs().

title: Prepare and Validate SHMI Input Data from an Excel Workbook — prepare_shmi_inputs
text: function stops informative errors : required sheets missing required columns missing crop chronology biologically impossible date overrides produce empty rotations

what: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: **SHMI** package provides complete, reproducible workflow computing Soil Health Management Index (SHMI) standardized Excel workbooks. SHMI composite indicator integrating four management sub-indices:

title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: Cover — seasonal plant presence Diversity — rotation-scale crop diversity (Hill numbers) Inverse Disturbance — mechanistic mixing-efficiency × depth metric Organic Inputs — amendments animal presence package includes: robust input validation harmonization biologically realistic crop-window processing fast vectorized daily-grid construction mechanistic disturbance modeling rotation-scale aggregation official national SHMI settings (locked mode) expert-mode overrides research scenario analysis

title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: complete SHMI workflow consists : Prepare inputs     prepare_shmi_inputs()     Reads validates Excel workbook, harmonizes crop windows,     constructs rotation bounds, generates daily grids. Compute SHMI     build_shmi()     Computes four sub-indices combines final SHMI score. Interpret results     returned object includes sub-indices scores, final SHMI values,     settings used, timestamp reproducibility.

title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: default, SHMI computed using official national settings (locked mode). Setting expert_mode = TRUE allows users override weights parameters, resulting SHMI values comparable national SHMI scale.

title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: SHMI outputs include: shmi_version — version SHMI algorithm timestamp — computation time settings_used — full list settings applied

title: SHMI: Soil Health Management Index — SHMI
text: prepare_shmi_inputs — read, validate, harmonize inputs build_shmi — compute SHMI scores compute_cover — cover sub-index compute_diversity — diversity sub-index compute_disturbance — inverse disturbance sub-index compute_orginput — organic inputs sub-index

title: Example SHMI dataset — shmi_example
text: small example dataset included SHMI package demonstrating minimal R-native workflow:

title: Example SHMI dataset — shmi_example
text: data frame X rows Y variables: MGT_combo Management unit identifier date Calendar date CD_name Crop mixture name crop_present 1 crop present, 0 otherwise SD_mixeff Mixing efficiency (present) SD_depth_cm Tillage depth cm (present) ... Additional variables depending example

title: Example SHMI dataset — shmi_example
text: “` data(shmi_example) inputs <- prepare_shmi_inputs(shmi_example) result <- build_shmi(inputs) “` dataset simplified, single-table representation SHMI input structure. intended quick examples, teaching, unit tests. full Excel-based workflow, use [`download_shmi_example()`] [`download_shmi_template()`].

what: Validate SHMI Excel Input File — validate_excel_input
title: Validate SHMI Excel Input File — validate_excel_input
text: Validates raw Excel file supplied `prepare_shmi_inputs()`. function checks required sheets, required columns, valid date formats, missing invalid `MGT_combo` values, malformed mixtures, species lookup consistency, rotation boundary completeness. designed fail early explicitly ingestion harmonization occurs.

title: Validate SHMI Excel Input File — validate_excel_input
Validate SHMI Excel Input File — validate_excel_input
text: path Character string. Path Excel file supplied user.

title: Validate SHMI Excel Input File — validate_excel_input
text: list : ok Logical. TRUE validation passed; FALSE otherwise. errors Character vector critical validation failures. warnings Character vector non-fatal issues. summary tibble summarizing sheet counts row counts.

title: Validate SHMI Input Tables — validate_shmi_input
text: Performs structural semantic validation input data list used `build_shmi()`. function checks required tables, required columns, valid data types, missing invalid values, duplicated rows, rotation boundary consistency. returns structured list containing validation status, error messages, warnings, summary key dataset properties. validator designed fail early explicitly critical issues detected (e.g., missing `MGT_combo`, malformed dates, duplicated daily rows). Non-fatal issues returned warnings. summary field counts, years, species richness, mixture counts, fallow days included diagnostic transparency.

title: Validate SHMI Input Tables — validate_shmi_input
text: dat named list SHMI input tables, typically produced `prepare_shmi_inputs()`. Must contain least: crop_harmonized tibble harmonized crop records     `MGT_combo`, `date`, `CD_name`. daily tibble daily crop presence `MGT_combo`, `date`,     `CD_name`, `crop_present`. rot_bounds tibble defining rotation start end dates     `MGT_combo`, columns `rot_start` `rot_end`.

title: Validate SHMI Input Tables — validate_shmi_input
text: list following elements: ok Logical. `TRUE` validation passed errors; `FALSE`     otherwise. errors Character vector critical validation failures. non-empty,     `build_shmi()` stop execution. warnings Character vector non-fatal issues. summary tibble summarizing key dataset properties (fields, years,     species, mixtures, fallow days).

title: Validate SHMI Input Tables — validate_shmi_input
text: function enforces SHMI input integrity checking: Presence required tables columns. missing `MGT_combo` values. date columns class `Date`. Rotation boundaries valid (`rot_start` ≤ `rot_end`). duplicated `(MGT_combo, date)` rows daily table. `crop_present` contains 0, 1, `NA`. Basic dataset diagnostics (fields, years, species richness, mixtures).

title: Validate SHMI Input Tables — validate_shmi_input
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dat <- prepare_shmi_inputs("path/to/input/folder") val <- validate_shmi_input(dat) if (!val$ok) stop("Validation failed:\n", paste(val$errors, collapse = "\n")) print(val$summary) } # }
